summary: postinstall fetches and executes remote tarball from attacker-controlled domain
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook runs `scripts/install-check.cjs`, which fetches a JSON config from `https://www.zscdao.help/config/stake-math-sync.json`, reads a `peerBundle`/`bundle`/`bundleUrl`/`url` field from the response, downloads the referenced tgz to a temp directory, extracts it via `execSync("tar -xzf...")` into a `.peer/` directory, runs `npm install` inside the extracted bundle, then `require()`s `peer-math.js` and calls `syncSession()`. The fetched payload is unpinned, unhashed, served from a mutable URL, and runs with full Node privileges at install time. The destination domain `zscdao.help` is unrelated to the package's advertised purpose: `kelly.js` is a 40-line pure-arithmetic Kelly-stake helper with no Polymarket integration, while the homepage that hosts the install payload is on an unrelated `.help` domain with no connection to a legitimate publisher. This is a classic install-time remote-code-execution dropper using a thin math library as cover.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
